transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports left shoulder pain for several weeks following backyard work.
*   The patient reports a history of right shoulder pain related to baseball playing.
*   The patient reports no numbness in the arm or hands.
*   The clinician assesses the patient’s right shoulder as having limited range of motion and tenderness at the greater tuberosity.
*   The clinician diagnoses the patient with rotator cuff tendinopathy.
*   The clinician orders an MRI of the right shoulder.
*   The clinician recommends physical therapy for six to eight weeks.
*   The clinician suggests considering a steroid injection if symptoms persist.
*   The patient reports taking Advil and icing the shoulder.
*   The patient reports foot soreness following yard work.
*   The patient reports trying ice and elevation for the feet.
